title: Choose your format
content:
Before you write a word, pick a structure. There are three, and the right one depends on your work history.

**Reverse-chronological** - lists your jobs newest first. This is the default and what most recruiters and ATS expect. Use it if you have a steady work history in the field you're applying to. If you're unsure, use this one.

**Functional (skills-based)** - leads with skills and groups achievements by theme instead of by job. It downplays dates. Recruiters are wary of it because it's often used to hide gaps, and many ATS parse it poorly. Use it rarely - only if you're changing careers hard and your titles don't tell the story.

**Combination** - a short skills/summary block up top, then a normal reverse-chronological history below. This is the practical middle ground for career changers and senior people: you frame your strengths first, but still give the dated history ATS and humans want.

**Bottom line:** default to reverse-chronological. Use combination if you need to reframe. Avoid pure functional unless you have a specific reason.

---
